title: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese

seo.title: Cantonese Steamed Fish: Authentic & Easy Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish with our easy recipe. Learn how to steam a whole fish perfectly with ginger, scallions, and soy sauce. Delicious!
longTailKeywords: how to make Cantonese Steamed Fish
Cantonese Steamed Fish step by step
best Cantonese Steamed Fish at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: how to steam whole fish Chinese style
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Freshly steamed whole fish Cantonese style, garnished with ginger, scallions, and cilantro, drizzled with hot oil.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the delicate flavors of authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish, a simple yet elegant dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Experience the delicate simplicity of Cantonese Steamed Whole Fish, a classic dish celebrated for its fresh flavors and tender texture. This recipe showcases the natural sweetness of the fish, enhanced by aromatic ginger, scallions, and a savory soy sauce dressing. It's an elegant and healthy meal perfect for family dinners or special occasions.

ingredients.item: Whole fish (e.g., branzino, snapper, sea bass)

ingredients.notes: half cut into 2-inch lengths, half julienned

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Rinse the whole fish thoroughly, pat it completely dry with paper towels. Make two to three diagonal scores on both sides of the fish (if it is a thick fish) to help it cook evenly. Rub a pinch of salt and white pepper all over the fish, inside and out. Place the 2-inch cut scallion pieces and ginger slices into the fish cavity and on top of the fish.

instructions.title: Set Up Steamer
instructions.text: Fill a wok or a large pot with water and bring it to a rolling boil over high heat. Place a heatproof plate or shallow dish that fits your fish and steamer into the steamer rack. Ensure the water level is below the rack so it does not touch the fish plate.

instructions.title: Steam the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the plate with the prepared fish onto the steamer rack. Cover the wok or pot tightly with a lid. Steam the fish for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of the fish. A good indicator that the fish is cooked is when the flesh turns opaque and flakes easily with a fork.

instructions.title: Drain and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the fish is cooked, carefully remove the steaming plate from the wok. Discard any accumulated liquid on the plate, as well as the cooked ginger and scallions from the fish. Arrange the fresh julienned ginger and scallion slivers evenly over the top of the steamed fish.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dressing
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the light soy sauce, sugar, white pepper, sesame oil, and hot water or chicken broth. Stir well until the sugar is completely dissolved. This will be your savory dressing for the fish.

instructions.title: Sizzle and Serve
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable or peanut oil in a small pan until it is smoking hot. Carefully pour the hot oil directly over the julienned ginger and scallions on the fish. This step releases their aromatic flavors. Immediately drizzle the prepared soy sauce mixture over the fish. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve the Cantonese Steamed Whole Fish immediately with steamed rice.

tips: For perfectly cooked fish, steam it just until the flesh turns opaque and flakes easily. Overcooking will result in dry, tough fish.
Always use a very fresh whole fish for the best flavor and texture. Look for clear eyes, red gills, and firm flesh. Any white fish like branzino, snapper, or sea bass works well.
Steamed fish is best enjoyed immediately after cooking. It does not store well as leftovers, as the delicate texture can become rubbery.
article: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a beloved Chinese Seafood dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
